rationale: In the theory section, the authors explicitly state that their work builds on findings that an initial difficult task leads to less effortful subsequent decision strategies, citing Pocheptsova et al. (2009). They cite it again when arguing that resource constraints trigger more intuitive processing, which motivates their hypotheses about 1/n# and 1/n$ behavior under larger assortments. This makes the citation part of the paper's core mechanism, not a passing background mention.
relation_description: The citing paper treats Pocheptsova et al. (2009) as key evidence that depleted cognitive resources shift choice toward simpler, less deliberative processing. It uses that logic as a conceptual foundation for explaining why larger retirement fund assortments increase heuristic allocation behavior.
